zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Ferrers於1413年3月出生在英國斯塔福爾哈特利 卡素時，她的父親Edmund Ferrers27歲，母親Elene de la Roche19歲。她於1434年之前與John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1487年1月29日在英國素福爾可登維其格也福利亞斯 普利奧利享年73歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾梅列瓦列。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Ferrers于1413年3月出生在英国斯塔福爾哈特利 卡素时，她的父亲Edmund Ferrers27岁，母亲Elene de la Roche19岁。她于1434年之前与John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1487年1月29日在英国素福爾可登維其格也福利亞斯 普利奧利享年73岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾梅列瓦列。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Ferrers was born in March 1413, in Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, England, her father, Edmund Ferrers, was 27 and her mother, Elene de la Roche, was 19. She married John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick before 1434, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 29 January 1487, in Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, England, at the age of 73, and was buried in Merevale, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Ferrers est née en mars 1413, à Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Ferrers, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elene de la Roche, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick avant 1434, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 29 janvier 1487, à Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterrée à Merevale, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Ferrers im März 1413 in Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Ferrers 27 und ihre Mutter Elene de la Roche 19. Sie hat vor 1434 in England, United Kingdom John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 29. Januar 1487 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, England und wurde in Merevale, Warwickshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ferrers è nata il nel marzo 1413 nel Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Edmund Ferrers, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Elene de la Roche, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick prima del 1434, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 29 gennaio 1487, nel Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, England, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolta a Merevale, Warwickshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Ferrersが1413年3月にイギリススタフォードシュアイアチャートリー カストルで生まれた時，父Edmund Ferrersは27歳，母Elene de la Rocheは19歳であった。1434年以前にイギリスイギリスでJohn Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1487年1月29日にイギリススフォルクダンウィチグレイフリアルズ プリオリーで73歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアメレベルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Ferrers(이)가 1413년 3월 영국 스태퍼드실르 처틀리 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Ferrers(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elene de la Roche(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1434년 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1487년 1월 29일 영국 수폴크 던위치 그리프리아르스 플리오리에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 머에발에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ferrers nasceu em março de 1413, em Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Ferrers, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Elene de la Roche, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick antes 1434, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 29 de janeiro de 1487, em Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 73 anos, e foi sepultada em Merevale, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Ferrers родилась марта 1413 г. в местности чартли кастл, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Ferrers было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elene de la Roche, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick перед 1434 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 29 января 1487 г. в местности грифрярс приори, дунвич, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности миревеил, варвиккшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Ferrers nació en marzo de 1413, en Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Ferrers, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Elene de la Roche, tenía 19 años. Se casó con John Beauchamp 1st Baron Beauchamp of Powick antes de 1434, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 29 de enero de 1487, en Greyfriars Priory, Dunwich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en Merevale, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
